units.title: Clinical Orientation
units.content:
Moodle Activity Type: Page or Lesson (informational) Section Name: Optional Clinical Support - Unit 1: Clinical Orientation Estimated Time: 15 minutes Required/Optional: Optional Certificate Gate: No Completion Type: Optional activity completion (view) Evidence Created: Optional view record (does not count toward CE completion) Source References: FINAL_UNIFIED_IMPLEMENTATION_BLUEPRINT.md (optional clinical support section); README.md

Learner-Facing Intro

Welcome to Optional Clinical Support

This optional section is here to help you feel more confident applying what you learned in the online theory modules. It includes orientation information, skills refresh resources, scheduling guidance, and optional practice activities.

This section is completely optional. You can access it before, during, or after completing the online theory course. Nothing here is required for your certificate.

Unit 1 Content: What Is This Clinical Support Section?

Learner Instructions:

This clinical support section provides:

Orientation to what optional skills refresh support is available.
Skills refresh menu - a list of skill areas where you can review technique and expectations.
Scheduling guidance - how to arrange optional practice support if your employer or facility offers it.
Optional confidence checks - low-stakes self-assessment activities to identify areas where you feel less confident.
Practice documentation support - templates and guides for practicing documentation skills.
RN/preceptor support signoff workflow - if your employer provides optional supervised practice, how that process works.
Help path - where to go if you have questions or need additional support.

None of these activities are graded. None are required. None are clinical hours.

Non-PHI Warning

FLAG Do not include real patient or resident names, dates of birth, medical record numbers, or any other identifying information in any practice documentation or scenario response. Use fictional names only. Examples: "Ms. Johnson," "Mr. Park," "Mrs. Chen."

Facilitator/Admin Notes
Unit 1 is purely informational. No grading, no gating, no completion tracking for CE purposes.
Ensure that the Moodle section label clearly states "Optional" and is visually distinct from required modules.
This unit should not be made a prerequisite for anything.
units.compliance_review_flag: Optional Clinical Support must remain non-credit, non-gating, not clinical-hour credit, and must not request PHI.

units.title: Skills Refresh Menu
units.content:
Moodle Activity Type: Page (with links to resource pages) Section Name: Optional Clinical Support - Unit 2: Skills Refresh Menu Estimated Time: Varies by skill selected (10-30 minutes per skill) Required/Optional: Optional Certificate Gate: No Completion Type: Optional activity completion (view) Evidence Created: Optional view record only Source References: NATP Modules 10-17 (as applicable to each skill); FINAL_UNIFIED_IMPLEMENTATION_BLUEPRINT.md

Learner-Facing Intro

This is your optional skills refresh menu. Each topic below links to a brief review page covering key points, common errors, and what to watch for in practice. You can review any topic at any time - before shifts, after completing the theory modules, or any time you want a quick reference.

These are reference resources only - not graded, not clinical hours, not required.

Skills Refresh Menu Topics
# Skill Topic Related Theory Module Estimated Review Time
1 Hand hygiene technique Module 1 10 min
2 Donning and doffing PPE Module 1 10 min
3 Safe transfer techniques (bed-to-chair) Module 4 15 min
4 Gait belt application and ambulation assist Module 4 10 min
5 Passive and active-assistive ROM Module 4 15 min
6 Fall prevention environment check Module 4 10 min
7 Feeding assistance and aspiration prevention positioning Module 5 15 min
8 Vital signs - blood pressure technique Module 5 15 min
9 Vital signs - pulse and respirations Module 5 10 min
10 Pressure injury skin inspection (bony prominences) Module 5 15 min
11 Repositioning technique and schedule Module 5 10 min
12 Objective documentation - writing a chart entry Module 6 15 min
13 Change-of-condition reporting using SBAR Module 6 10 min
14 Dementia communication - de-escalation Module 3 15 min
15 End-of-life comfort measures Module 3 15 min
16 Abuse recognition - what to look for Module 2 10 min
17 Emergency choking response review Module 4 10 min
18 Cultural sensitivity in daily care Module 3 10 min
Sample Skills Refresh Page - Topic 7: Feeding Assistance and Aspiration Prevention

Learner Instructions:

This is a brief optional review. It does not replace hands-on practice under supervision.

Key points to remember:

Position the resident upright (60-90 degrees) before starting the meal and keep them upright for at least 30 minutes after.
Sit at the resident's eye level - do not stand over them.
Offer small bites (half-teaspoon size). Give one bite at a time.
Allow adequate time between bites. Do not rush.
Watch for: coughing, gagging, wet or gurgling voice, color change, difficulty breathing.
If thickened liquids are ordered, use the ordered consistency for ALL liquids - water, juice, coffee, broth.
Never feed a resident who is drowsy, reclined, or unresponsive.
If you observe any signs of aspiration: stop feeding, keep the resident upright, call for help.

Common errors to avoid:

Feeding too fast.
Using thin liquids when thickened are ordered.
Standing instead of sitting at eye level.
Not checking the diet order before starting.

Source: NATP Module 11 (Nutrition), Objectives 4-5

Facilitator/Admin Notes
Each skills refresh topic should be a separate Moodle page within the optional support section.
Resources should link to the relevant theory module lesson for deeper review.
No quiz or graded activity should be embedded in the skills refresh menu.

units.title: Scheduling Guidance
units.content:
Moodle Activity Type: Page (informational) Section Name: Optional Clinical Support - Unit 3: Scheduling Guidance Estimated Time: 10 minutes Required/Optional: Optional Certificate Gate: No Completion Type: Optional activity completion (view) Evidence Created: Optional view record only Source References: FINAL_UNIFIED_IMPLEMENTATION_BLUEPRINT.md; README.md

Learner-Facing Intro

If your employer offers optional supervised practice support, this page explains how to arrange it. This is separate from your online CE theory course and is not required for your certificate.

Unit 3 Content

Optional skills practice support availability depends entirely on your employer. This course does not provide or arrange clinical practice time. If you are interested in optional supervised skills practice:

Step 1: Talk to your supervisor, charge nurse, or staff development coordinator at your facility.

Step 2: Ask whether optional skills refresh support is available during orientation, scheduled practice sessions, or alongside regular shifts.

Step 3: If your employer has enrolled you in this course and provides optional preceptor or RN support, ask for the contact name and how to schedule.

Step 4: If you have completed optional confidence checks (Unit 4) and identified areas where you feel less confident, bring that list to your scheduling conversation.

What this course does NOT provide:

Scheduled clinical practice time
Clinical supervision
Clinical-hour credit
Any form of certification for hands-on skills

Reminder: Optional practice support is not tracked in this Moodle course for CE purposes and does not appear on your certificate.

Facilitator/Admin Notes
If the organization has a specific scheduling process for optional clinical support, insert the contact name and process in Step 3 above before deploying this page.
Do not name this section anything that implies clinical credit or required practicum.

units.title: Optional Confidence Checks
units.content:
Moodle Activity Type: Questionnaire or Survey (non-graded, optional) Section Name: Optional Clinical Support - Unit 4: Optional Confidence Checks Estimated Time: 20-30 minutes Required/Optional: Optional Certificate Gate: No Completion Type: Optional (no completion tracking for CE) Evidence Created: Optional questionnaire response (not graded, not stored as CE evidence) Source References: NATP Modules 10-17; FINAL_UNIFIED_IMPLEMENTATION_BLUEPRINT.md

Learner-Facing Intro

Optional Confidence Checks - Not Graded, Not Required

These self-rating activities help you identify skill areas where you feel confident and areas where you might want additional practice or review.

Your responses are for your own personal development. They are not submitted for a grade. They do not affect your certificate. They are not clinical-hour credit.

Full 20-Item Optional Confidence Check List

(Each item includes a self-rating scale of 1-4: 1=Not confident, 2=Somewhat confident, 3=Confident, 4=Very confident)

Check 1 - Vital Signs: Blood Pressure
Topic: Blood pressure measurement
Source Module: Module 5 / NATP Module 10 (Vital Signs)
Learner Prompt: How confident are you in your ability to measure blood pressure accurately using a manual cuff?
Self-Rating Scale: 1 = Not confident / 2 = Somewhat confident / 3 = Confident / 4 = Very confident
Scenario: A fictional resident "Mr. Hassan" has a care plan requiring BP every shift. You are assigned to take it. Rate your confidence in positioning the cuff, inflating correctly, and reading the result accurately.
Recommended Response: Cuff at heart level, proper cuff size selected, resident seated or lying with arm relaxed, inflate cuff to 180 mmHg or 30 mmHg above the last known systolic reading, deflate slowly at 2-3 mmHg per second, note the first Korotkoff sound (systolic) and the point where sound disappears (diastolic), record both numbers, date, time, and which arm used.
Feedback: If you rated 1 or 2, review Module 5 Lesson 5.4 and the Skills Refresh Menu Topic 8 before your next shift.
Recommended Refresh Resource: Module 5 Lesson 5.4 (Vital Signs); Skills Refresh Menu Topic 8 (Blood Pressure Technique)
Not Graded: Yes
Not Certificate-Gated: Yes
Check 2 - Vital Signs: Pulse and Respirations
Topic: Pulse and respiration measurement
Source Module: Module 5 / NATP Module 10 (Vital Signs)
Learner Prompt: How confident are you in counting a radial pulse for a full 60 seconds and counting respirations without alerting the resident?
Self-Rating Scale: 1 = Not confident / 2 = Somewhat confident / 3 = Confident / 4 = Very confident
Scenario: A fictional resident "Mrs. Okafor" is sitting in her chair. You need to take her pulse and respirations. Rate your confidence in locating the radial pulse, using the correct fingers, counting for a full minute, and transitioning to respirations without the resident noticing.
Recommended Response: Use index and middle fingers on the thumb side of the wrist. Count beats for a full 60 seconds. Note rate, rhythm, and strength. Without announcing it, observe chest rises for a full 60 seconds while appearing to still take the pulse. Record both findings.
Feedback: If you rated 1 or 2, review Module 5 Lesson 5.4 Screen 5.4.3 and Skills Refresh Menu Topic 9.
Recommended Refresh Resource: Module 5 Lesson 5.4; Skills Refresh Menu Topic 9 (Pulse and Respirations)
Not Graded: Yes
Not Certificate-Gated: Yes
Check 3 - Nutrition and Feeding
Topic: Feeding assistance technique
Source Module: Mo

units.title: Practice Documentation Support
units.content:
Moodle Activity Type: Page (with downloadable practice templates) Section Name: Optional Clinical Support - Unit 5: Practice Documentation Support Estimated Time: 20-30 minutes Required/Optional: Optional Certificate Gate: No Completion Type: Optional activity completion (view) Evidence Created: Optional view record only Source References: NATP Module 15 (Observation and Charting); FINAL_UNIFIED_IMPLEMENTATION_BLUEPRINT.md

Learner-Facing Intro

Optional Practice Documentation Support - Not Graded, Not Required

Good documentation is a skill that improves with practice. This unit provides optional documentation practice templates and sample entries to help you build confidence in writing objective, complete chart entries.

These are practice exercises only. They are not submitted for a grade. They do not affect your certificate. Do not use real resident names or any identifying information in any practice activity.

Non-PHI Warning

FLAG All practice documentation entries must use fictional names only. Do not include real resident names, dates of birth, room numbers that could identify real individuals, medical record numbers, or any other personally identifiable information. Suggested fictional names for practice: Ms. Johnson, Mr. Park, Mrs. Chen, Mr. Brooks, Mrs. Torres.

Practice Template 1 - Routine Care Entry

Instructions: Using the scenario below, write a complete objective chart entry in the space provided (paper practice or a blank word-processing document).

Scenario: A fictional resident "Ms. Johnson" received a bed bath, oral care, and was repositioned to her right side at 0900. She consumed 100% of breakfast. BP was 126/78, pulse 74 regular. She stated: "My back feels better when I'm on this side."

Sample Correct Entry:

0900 - Bed bath and oral care provided. Resident repositioned to right lateral position. BP 126/78 right arm, seated. Pulse 74 regular at radial. Resident consumed 100% of breakfast. Resident stated: "My back feels better when I'm on this side." No skin breakdown observed at bony prominences during care. Tolerated all activities without distress. - [CNA Name], CNA, [Date]

Common errors to avoid:

Writing "seems comfortable" instead of objective observations.
Omitting vital signs units (write "126/78 mmHg" or "74 bpm").
Documenting care before it was completed.
Omitting signature, title, and date.
Practice Template 2 - Change of Condition Entry

Instructions: Using the scenario below, write a complete change-of-condition report entry.

Scenario: At 1400, a fictional resident "Mr. Park" is observed sitting in his chair, looking pale. He states: "I feel really dizzy." BP is 86/54 (baseline 128/80). Respirations 18. You notified the charge nurse at 1402.

Sample Correct Entry:

1400 - Observed resident sitting in chair, appearing pale. Resident stated: "I feel really dizzy." BP 86/54 right arm sitting (baseline 128/80). Respirations 18 and regular. Charge Nurse Smith notified at 1402. Resident kept in chair until nurse assessment. Side rails raised on chair, call light within reach. - [CNA Name], CNA, [Date]

Practice Template 3 - Refusal of Care Entry

Instructions: Using the scenario below, write a refusal-of-care documentation entry.

Scenario: A fictional resident "Mrs. Chen" refused her scheduled bath at 0800. She stated: "I'm too tired today." You offered to reschedule for later.

Sample Correct Entry:

0800 - Resident refused scheduled bath. Resident stated: "I'm too tired today." Refusal respected. Wi

units.title: RN/Preceptor Support Signoff Workflow
units.content:
Moodle Activity Type: Page (informational workflow) Section Name: Optional Clinical Support - Unit 6: RN/Preceptor Support Signoff Workflow Estimated Time: 10 minutes Required/Optional: Optional Certificate Gate: No Completion Type: Optional activity completion (view) Evidence Created: Optional view record only - this signoff is NOT CE evidence and does NOT appear on the CE certificate Source References: FINAL_UNIFIED_IMPLEMENTATION_BLUEPRINT.md; README.md

Learner-Facing Intro

If your employer offers optional supervised skills practice with an RN or preceptor, this page describes how that process typically works. This is entirely separate from your online CE theory course.

Important: Any optional signoff from an RN or preceptor is a workplace support document only. It is NOT a certificate of clinical hours. It does NOT satisfy California CNA renewal clinical-hour requirements. It does NOT appear on your online CE certificate.

Unit 6 Content

What is the optional RN/preceptor signoff?

Some employers provide a structured opportunity for CNAs to practice skills with a supervising RN or preceptor after completing the online theory course. If your employer offers this:

The RN or preceptor observes you performing specific skills.
They provide feedback and guidance.
They may sign a workplace acknowledgment form confirming they observed your practice.

This is workplace-level support, not clinical-hour credit for CNA renewal.

Typical workflow (if your employer offers this support):

Complete the online CE theory course (all 7 required modules).
Review your optional confidence check results (Unit 4) and identify skills for optional practice.
Contact your supervisor or staff development coordinator to arrange optional supervised practice time.
Practice the skill with the RN or preceptor during an arranged session.
The RN or preceptor completes the optional observation form if provided by your employer. This form is for your employer's records - it is not submitted to this course.
Keep a copy for your own records if desired.

What this signoff does NOT do:

It does not complete your CNA renewal clinical hours.
It does not generate a CE certificate.
It does not replace CDPH-required clinical competency processes.
It is not submitted to or tracked by this Moodle course.
Sample Optional Observation Acknowledgment Form (Employer Use Only)

OPTIONAL SKILLS PRACTICE ACKNOWLEDGMENT FORM (Employer workplace document - not a CE certificate - not for regulatory submission)

CNA Name: _______________________ Date of Practice Session: _______________________ Skill(s) Observed: _______________________ Supervising RN/Preceptor Name: _______________________ Facility: _______________________

Notes: _______________________

RN/Preceptor Signature: _______________________ Date: _______________________

THIS DOCUMENT IS A WORKPLACE SUPPORT RECORD ONLY. IT IS NOT CALIFORNIA CNA RENEWAL CLINICAL-HOUR CREDIT.

Facilitator/Admin Notes
The optional observation form is a sample. Employers should adapt it to their own documentation standards.
Under no circumstances should this form be presented as clinical-hour documentation for CNA renewal.
Do not make this unit a prerequisite for any other activity in the course.
Do not store submitted signoff forms as CE evidence in Moodle.

units.title: Support Follow-up and Help Path
units.content:
Moodle Activity Type: Page (informational) Section Name: Optional Clinical Support - Unit 7: Support Follow-up and Help Path Estimated Time: 5 minutes Required/Optional: Optional Certificate Gate: No Completion Type: Optional activity completion (view) Evidence Created: Optional view record only Source References: FINAL_UNIFIED_IMPLEMENTATION_BLUEPRINT.md; README.md

Learner-Facing Intro

Support Follow-up and Help Path

This page tells you who to contact and what to do if you have questions, technical problems, or need additional support after completing this course.

Unit 7 Content

If you have questions about your CE certificate:

Contact the course administrator at your facility or the course provider.
Check your Moodle course completion report to confirm all gates are satisfied.

If you have questions about your full CNA renewal requirements:

Contact your employer's HR or staff development department.
Contact the California Department of Public Health (CDPH) Aide and Technician Certification Section (ATCS).
[Insert current CDPH contact information per your organization's policy - do not fabricate contact details.]

If you experienced a technical problem during the course:

Contact your Moodle system administrator or IT support.
Do not re-attempt the final exam without contacting support first if a technical issue occurred during the exam.

If you want additional skills practice support:

Talk to your supervisor or charge nurse about optional supervised practice opportunities at your facility.
Review the Skills Refresh Menu (Unit 2) and Confidence Checks (Unit 4) to identify priority areas.

If you have concerns about course content:

Contact the course administrator or staff development coordinator.
Module 1 (Infection Control) and skin integrity content are flagged for SME review - if you have questions about specific practices, confirm with your facility's infection control officer or clinical educator.

Reminder:

This course provides partial California CNA continuing education credit - online theory hours only. It does not complete your full renewal requirements. Optional clinical support activities are not California renewal clinical-hour credit.

Facilitator/Admin Notes
Insert current CDPH contact information before course deployment. Do not leave placeholder text visible to learners.
This unit should remain accessible to learners after course completion for reference.
If the organization has a dedicated learner support contact, add their name and contact information to this page.

confidence_checks.title: Vital Signs: Blood Pressure
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in measuring blood pressure accurately with a manual cuff and reporting the result correctly?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mr. Hassan" requires BP every shift. His arm is at his side when you enter. Rate your confidence in the full measurement procedure.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Position the resident seated or lying with the arm supported at heart level. Select the correct cuff size. Apply the cuff smoothly 2-3 cm above the antecubital fossa. Inflate to 180 mmHg or 30 mmHg above the last known systolic. Deflate at 2-3 mmHg per second. Record the first Korotkoff sound as systolic and the disappearance of sound as diastolic. Document the reading, date, time, arm used, and position. Report any reading significantly outside his baseline to the licensed nurse immediately.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 5 Lesson 5.4; Skills Refresh Menu Topic 8
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Vital Signs: Pulse and Respirations
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in counting a radial pulse and transitioning silently to a respiration count without alerting the resident?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mrs. Okafor" is in bed. You need pulse and respirations. Rate your confidence in the procedure.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Place index and middle fingers on the thumb side of the wrist. Count beats for a full 60 seconds. Note rate, rhythm (regular or irregular), and strength (strong, weak, bounding, or thready). Without announcing the change, keep your fingers in position and observe chest rises for a full 60 seconds. Record pulse rate and rhythm, respiration rate and depth, and note any irregularity. Report pulse below 60 or above 100, or respirations below 12 or above 20, to the licensed nurse.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 5 Lesson 5.4 Screen 5.4.3; Skills Refresh Menu Topic 9
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Nutrition and Feeding Assistance
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in providing feeding assistance safely and maintaining the resident's dignity throughout?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mrs. Park" requires full feeding assistance. Her diet is pureed with nectar-thick liquids. Rate your confidence in the complete feeding procedure.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Check the diet card before beginning. Position resident upright at 60-90 degrees. Wash hands and apply gloves per facility policy. Sit at the resident's eye level. Describe each food. Offer half-teaspoon bites. Allow full swallowing time between bites. Offer thickened fluids between bites. Watch for coughing, gagging, wet voice, or color changes. If any aspiration sign occurs, stop immediately, keep the resident upright, and call the nurse. Keep the resident upright for 30 minutes after the meal. Document intake percentage and fluid amount.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 5 Lesson 5.2; Skills Refresh Menu Topic 7
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Aspiration Risk Recognition
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in recognizing aspiration warning signs and responding correctly?
confidence_checks.scenario: You are feeding a fictional resident "Mr. Obi." After swallowing, he coughs and his voice sounds wet. Rate your confidence in your immediate response.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Stop feeding immediately. Do not offer any more food or liquid. Keep the resident upright. Stay with the resident and observe for continued coughing, labored breathing, or cyanosis. Call for the licensed nurse. Report: "Mr. Obi coughed after swallowing pureed food at 1215 and his voice sounds wet. I stopped feeding and he is upright." Document objective observations including time, what was being eaten, and what you observed. Do not resume feeding until the nurse assesses the resident.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 5 Lesson 5.2 (Aspiration Prevention)
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Dehydration Recognition and Response
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in recognizing dehydration signs and taking the correct steps?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mrs. Chen" is more confused than yesterday. Her mouth looks dry and her urine was dark amber this morning. Rate your confidence in recognizing and responding.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Recognize dry mouth, dark urine, and new/worsening confusion as potential dehydration indicators. Within dietary restrictions per the care plan, offer fluids. Do not diagnose. Report immediately to the licensed nurse using SBAR: Situation - Mrs. Chen has worsening confusion and signs of possible dehydration. Background - dry mouth and dark amber urine noted this morning. Assessment - possible dehydration. Recommendation - please assess. Document findings and nurse notification with exact times.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 5 Lesson 5.1 (Hydration and Dehydration)
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Skin Integrity Inspection
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in performing a systematic skin inspection at bony prominences during routine care?
confidence_checks.scenario: You are performing morning care for a fictional bedridden resident "Mr. Reeves." Rate your confidence in systematically inspecting his skin.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Inspect the sacrum, heels, hips (greater trochanter), elbows, shoulder blades, back of head, ears, and ankles during bathing and repositioning. Look for non-blanchable redness, warmth, discoloration, breakdown, blisters, or skin tears. Press on any red area - if it does not turn white (non-blanchable), report immediately. Document location, size estimate, color, temperature, and the resident's report of pain or tenderness. Report all findings to the licensed nurse. Do not attempt to stage wounds - that is the licensed nurse's role.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 5 Lesson 5.3; Skills Refresh Menu Topic 10
confidence_checks.sme_review_flag: SME/source review required for skin integrity content.
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Pressure Injury Prevention
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in carrying out a complete pressure injury prevention routine for a high-risk resident?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mrs. Williams" is immobile, incontinent, and poorly nourished. Rate your confidence in knowing the full prevention routine.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Reposition at minimum every 2 hours - document each turn with position and time. Change soiled linens and briefs promptly. Apply moisture barrier cream as ordered. Keep linens smooth and wrinkle-free. Elevate heels per care plan. Use specialty mattress or pressure-relieving devices as ordered. Encourage fluid and nutritional intake. Inspect skin at bony prominences during every care episode. Lift - do not drag - during repositioning. Report any new redness, warmth, or skin breakdown immediately.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 5 Lesson 5.3; Skills Refresh Menu Topics 10 and 11
confidence_checks.sme_review_flag: SME/source review required for skin integrity content.
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Body Mechanics
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in consistently applying correct body mechanics during all physical care activities?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mr. Davis" needs to be repositioned up in bed. He can provide minimal assistance. Rate your confidence in positioning yourself and using the correct technique.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Lower the bed to working height. Lower the side rail. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, one foot slightly forward, knees bent. Engage core. Keep the resident close to your body. Use a draw sheet if available. Get a second person if needed per the care plan. Lift - do not drag. Pivot feet to change direction - do not twist. Restore bed position and raise side rail when complete.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 4 Lesson 4.1; Skills Refresh Menu Topic 3
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Range of Motion (ROM)
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in performing passive range of motion exercises safely for a resident with limited mobility?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mrs. Flores" has a PROM order for bilateral upper extremities. Rate your confidence in the correct technique.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Check the care plan for ordered joints and movements. Support the limb above and below the joint at all times. Move slowly, gently, and smoothly. Stop immediately at the first sign of resistance or any indication of pain. Watch the resident's facial expression throughout. Work from proximal (shoulder) to distal (fingers). Perform each movement the number of times ordered. Document completion, resident tolerance, and any reports of pain or new limitation.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 4 Lesson 4.3; Skills Refresh Menu Topic 5
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Ambulation and Fall Prevention
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in assisting a resident to walk safely using a gait belt and preventing a fall if the resident becomes unsteady?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mr. Brooks" has left-side weakness and a care plan for supervised ambulation with gait belt. Rate your confidence in the complete ambulation assist procedure.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Ensure non-skid footwear. Apply gait belt snugly over clothing at the waist. Stand slightly behind and to the left (weaker) side. Grasp the belt with an underhand grip. Walk at the resident's pace - never rush. Encourage looking forward. If unsteady, provide firm support via the gait belt. If falling is unavoidable, protect the resident's head, bend your knees, and guide them to the floor in a controlled descent. Call for help. Document ambulation distance, tolerance, and any incidents.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 4 Lesson 4.2; Skills Refresh Menu Topics 4 and 6
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Emergency Response
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in recognizing a medical emergency and knowing your first steps?
confidence_checks.scenario: You find a fictional resident "Mrs. Adams" unresponsive in her chair, not breathing normally. Rate your confidence in your immediate response.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Call out for help loudly. Activate emergency response - use the call light or send someone to alert the nurse immediately. Note the time. Check for breathing and a pulse. Know the resident's code status before acting. If the resident has no DNR and is not breathing: begin CPR if you are trained and certified, until help arrives. If the resident has a DNR: do not initiate CPR, keep them comfortable, stay with them, and wait for the licensed nurse. Provide a clear SBAR report to the nurse on arrival.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 4 Lesson 4.5; facility emergency code reference
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Choking Response
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in recognizing a choking emergency and performing abdominal thrusts correctly?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mr. Tanaka" is at dinner. He suddenly cannot speak, is turning red, and grabs his throat. Rate your confidence in your immediate response.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Ask "Are you choking?" - if they cannot speak, cough, or breathe, act immediately. Call for help while beginning the Heimlich. Stand behind the resident. Place one foot between their feet for stability. Make a fist with one hand just above the navel, below the sternum (xiphoid process). Grasp the fist with the other hand. Deliver firm upward abdominal thrusts. Repeat until the obstruction is expelled or the resident becomes unconscious. If unconscious: lower to the floor, activate emergency response, begin CPR per training, and look in the mouth before each rescue breath.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 4 Lesson 4.5; Skills Refresh Menu Topic 17
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Dementia Communication
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in using validation, redirection, and de-escalation with a resident who is agitated due to dementia?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mrs. Petrov" is agitated at 6 PM, saying she needs to go home. Rate your confidence in responding therapeutically.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Approach from the front. Make eye contact. Use her name. Speak slowly and softly in short sentences. Do not argue or correct. Validate: "You want to be home with your family - I understand." Redirect: "Tell me about your family - what are their names?" Offer a sensory cue (warm drink, light snack, gentle walk). Remove overstimulating stimuli if present (TV noise, bright lights). Document the behavior and the intervention used. Notify the nurse if agitation escalates or is new in presentation.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 3 Lesson 3.2; Skills Refresh Menu Topic 14
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Death and Dying Comfort Care
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in providing appropriate physical and emotional comfort care to a resident who is actively dying?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mr. Rivera" is on hospice, unresponsive, with Cheyne-Stokes breathing and mottled hands. His family is present. Rate your confidence in what to do.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Keep the resident clean, dry, and positioned for comfort per the care plan. Provide mouth care with a moistened swab and lip balm as ordered. Reposition gently to prevent pressure. Keep the room quiet and calm. Speak softly to the resident - hearing may be the last sense to go. Offer the family a chair, water, and emotional presence. Do not predict when death will occur. Report all changes in breathing, color, or responsiveness to the licensed nurse. Document care and family presence. Take care of yourself after this shift.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 3 Lesson 3.4; Skills Refresh Menu Topic 15
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Abuse Reporting
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in recognizing suspected abuse and following your mandated reporting obligations correctly?
confidence_checks.scenario: During morning care for a fictional resident "Mrs. Johnson," you notice a new bruise on her left upper arm and she flinches when you touch her arm gently. She says nothing. Rate your confidence in knowing what to do.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Ensure the resident's immediate safety. Do not confront any suspected staff member. Do not investigate. Report immediately to the licensed nurse: describe the bruise (location, approximate size, color), the resident's reaction (flinched at touch), and that there was no bruise documented yesterday. Document your objective observations only. Follow facility policy for abuse reporting (incident report, SOC 341 if applicable). Know you are legally protected when reporting in good faith. You do not need certainty - reasonable suspicion is sufficient.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 2 Lesson 2.4; Skills Refresh Menu Topic 16
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Objective Documentation
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in writing an objective, complete, and legally appropriate documentation entry?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mr. Park" ate 30% of lunch, told you his stomach is bothering him, and appeared more fatigued than baseline. Rate your confidence in writing the correct entry.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: "1200 - Resident consumed approximately 30% of lunch. Drank 180 mL apple juice (thickened to nectar per order). Resident stated: 'My stomach is bothering me.' Appeared fatigued - decreased interaction during meal, eyes closing intermittently. Reported to Charge Nurse at 1215. Awaiting further instruction." Sign with name, CNA, date, and time. Use the resident's exact words in quotes. Document only what was observed - no assumptions or opinions.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 6 Lesson 6.2; Skills Refresh Menu Topic 12; Clinical Support Unit 5 Practice Template 1
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Change-of-Condition Reporting
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in recognizing a reportable change of condition and organizing your report using SBAR?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mrs. Rivera" was alert this morning. At 2 PM she is confused, cannot state her name, and has a temperature of 100.1F. Rate your confidence in composing your SBAR report.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Situation: "I'm calling about Mrs. Rivera in Room 14 - she is acutely confused." Background: "She was alert and oriented at 0800. She has a history of recurrent UTIs." Assessment: "She cannot state her name, her temperature is 100.1F oral, BP 118/74, pulse 88 regular. She is picking at her clothing." Recommendation: "I believe she needs to be assessed - possible UTI or other medical change." Document the full report and the nurse's response with times.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 6 Lesson 6.3; Skills Refresh Menu Topic 13; Clinical Support Unit 5 Practice Template 2
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Scope of Practice
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in identifying tasks that are within your scope and declining tasks that are outside it?
confidence_checks.scenario: A licensed nurse, managing a second emergency down the hall, asks you to crush a medication and mix it into a fictional resident's food. Rate your confidence in knowing how to respond.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Decline politely and clearly: "I am not authorized to prepare or administer medications - that is outside my CNA scope. I will let you know the medication still needs to be given when you are available." Do not attempt the task under any circumstances. Document the situation and your response. This boundary exists regardless of urgency or who made the request.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 6 Lesson 6.4 (CNA Scope of Practice)
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: No-PHI Documentation
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in ensuring all your documentation and communications comply with HIPAA and do not expose resident PHI?
confidence_checks.scenario: A coworker mentions posting a situation from their shift on social media using only the room number. They ask you if that is acceptable. Rate your confidence in knowing the correct answer.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: This likely violates HIPAA. Any information - including room number combined with a clinical description - that could reasonably identify a resident must not be posted on social media, including private accounts. The correct response is to advise the coworker to delete the post, review the facility's social media policy, and notify their supervisor if in doubt. Document the conversation if you feel it warrants a report to the supervisor.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 6 Lesson 6.2; Module 2 Lesson 2.5
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Resident Rights in Daily Care
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in recognizing and honoring resident rights during a busy shift, including when a resident refuses care?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Ms. Torres" refuses her bath this morning. She says she just doesn't want one today. You are behind schedule. Rate your confidence in handling this correctly.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Respect her refusal - residents have the right to refuse any care. Do not pressure, threaten, or argue. Offer to come back later. Document: "0830 - Resident declined bath. Stated: 'I just don't want one today.' Refusal respected. Offered to return at 1300. Charge nurse notified per policy." Return at the offered time. If refusal is repeated or causes clinical concern, the nurse may need to assess.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 2 Lesson 2.1 (Resident Rights Foundation)
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

confidence_checks.title: Professional Boundaries
confidence_checks.prompt: How confident are you in maintaining professional boundaries, including when a resident or family member offers you a gift?
confidence_checks.scenario: A fictional resident "Mr. Carter" offers you $50 at the holidays and says it is just between the two of you. Rate your confidence in responding correctly.
confidence_checks.recommended_response: Decline respectfully: "That is so kind of you, Mr. Carter. It truly means a lot to hear that you feel well cared for. I am not able to accept money or gifts, but I appreciate your generosity." Document the offer and your response per facility policy. Notify the charge nurse. Accepting any gift - regardless of amount - is a professional boundary violation and may constitute financial abuse if a pattern develops.
confidence_checks.refresh_resource: Module 2 Lesson 2.5 (Professional Boundaries); Module 6 Lesson 6.5
confidence_checks.compliance_review_flag: Do not store as CE evidence; do not label as competency validation.

clinical_support.time_model_notes: Optional Clinical Support is optional, non-credit, non-gating, and never counts toward the 720 required theory instructional minutes. No clinical-hour credit is claimed.

dashboard.title: CNA CE Theory Portal
dashboard.summary: A structured 12-hour online theory program for California CNA continuing education, with identity acknowledgement, required theory modules, assessment gates, no-PHI safeguards, and audit-ready status tracking.
dashboard.compliance_notice: This course provides partial online CE theory only. It does not complete full California CNA renewal by itself. Learners remain responsible for total renewal hours, annual minimums, online-hour limits, and work/practice documentation. Optional Clinical Support is separate, optional, non-credit, and non-gating.
dashboard.theory_card: Required theory modules total 720 minutes / 12 hours: Orientation, Infection Control, Resident Rights, Dementia and Communication, Mobility and Safety, Nutrition/Skin/Vitals, Documentation/Scope, and Final Review/Assessment.
dashboard.clinical_card: Optional Clinical Support is for professional confidence only. It is not clinical-hour credit, not required, and never a certificate gate.
dashboard.audit_card: No PHI may be entered in course activities. Active-time and certificate surfaces remain demo/MVP until validated and approved.

module0.title: Identity and Compliance Orientation
module0.intro: Review the mandatory course boundaries before starting required theory. This orientation records identity, online-cap, no-PHI, and final readiness acknowledgements.

acknowledgements.text: I certify that my legal CNA identity matches the credential entered above, and that I alone will complete this CE training program.
No-PHI Safeguards Active: I will not enter real Protected Health Information (PHI), resident records, facility identifiers, or actual patient identifiers anywhere in this portal.
I understand this course provides partial online theory CE only. It does not provide clinical hours and does not, by itself, complete California CNA renewal.

moduleAssessment.title: Module 1 Knowledge Check
moduleAssessment.summary: Verify understanding of ContentV2 Infection Control and PPE concepts. Correct answer keys are hidden during and after completion.
moduleAssessment.remediation: Review infection-control source cards, standard precautions, hand hygiene, PPE, reporting signs, and linen/environmental safety before retaking.

final.title: Course Final Assessment
final.summary: A course-wide assessment across required theory topics. Correct answers and rationales are scored internally and are not revealed in learner-facing final results.
final.no_key_notice: To preserve assessment integrity, the final exam does not reveal correct answers during or after submission. Active-time remains demo/MVP unless the validated engine is implemented.
final.pass_body: You met the final assessment threshold. Continue to the certificate gate status page. Production certificate issuance remains disabled pending required approvals.
final.fail_body: Your score was below the required threshold. Review the related module topics and retake when ready. Correct answer keys remain locked.

certificate.intro: Certificate surfaces remain locked unless required gates are complete. Production issuance stays disabled at all times in this preview.
certificate.affidavit_text: I attest that I personally completed the required online theory activities in this portal. Draft wording only; e-signature method and legal language require approval.
certificate.ready_body: All required learner steps are complete. Production certificate issuance remains disabled until approval metadata, NAC#, approved wording, affidavit method, and active-time validation are configured.
certificate.locked_body: Complete legal verification, required theory study, final assessment, active-time, and the draft affidavit to unlock the mock certificate preview.
certificate.restriction: Official certificate downloading is disabled in preview environments. Real validation files may only be generated after approval metadata, provider identification, certificate wording, affidavit method, and all gates are complete.

clinicalHub.title: Clinical Scenario Support Hub
clinicalHub.warning: This area is optional and for professional confidence only. It provides zero clinical CE hours, does not gate required theory progress, does not fulfill hands-on renewal requirements, and must never request PHI.

scenarios.title: Skills Refresh Menu
scenarios.body: Review optional skill-reference topics tied to theory modules. These resources are not graded, not clinical hours, and not certificate gates.

scenarios.title: Optional Confidence Checks
scenarios.body: Use low-stakes self-ratings to identify topics for personal review. These checks are not competency validation and are not stored as CE evidence.

clinicalHub.documentation_warning: STOP: Do not enter PHI, real resident names, room numbers, facility data, medical record numbers, dates of birth, or actual case details. Use fictional practice notes only.

rules: Instructional minutes inherit from the ContentV1 syllabus allocation; the 720 total is not recomputed from narration or card count.
Narration, reading/review, interaction/challenge/remediation, and assessment minutes are tracked separately.
Module assessments and the final exam are excluded from the 720 instructional total.
Optional Clinical Support never counts toward the 720 required theory minutes.
Content-depth gaps are reported, not padded; SME/source-repair flags are preserved.
No clinical-hour credit is claimed; production certificate remains disabled; no PHI.
